name: Santander-Mediterranean Greenway: Valcorba - Ciria
name_original: Ciria desde Gómara — circular por la Vía Verde Santander-Mediterráneo
description: Cycle the 30.4-mile (49.0 km) Ciria from Gómara circular route on the Santander-Mediterranean Greenway, a moderate touring adventure.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Gómara offers tranquil routes through expansive agricultural plains, characterized by cereal cultivation and open horizons. The terrain is generally flat or gently rolling, making it suitable for relaxed cycling. Low-lying mountain ranges such as the Sierra de Costanazo, La Pica, or Toranzo provide a distant backdrop. The region also features historical elements like castles and Romanesque churches, integrated into the rural landscape. Best touring cycling routes around Gómara The most popular touring cycling route is Santander-Mediterráneo Greenway: Soria Loop, a 39.4 miles (63.3 km) trail that takes 4 hours 31 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows a section of the historic greenway through the Soria region. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Santander-Mediterranean Greenway: Valcorba - Ciria, a moderate 30.4 miles (49.0 km) path. This route traverses varied terrain, including sections near Ciria known for karst plateaus. Local touring cyclists also love the Almenar de Soria, Ólvega, and Matalebreras Loop, a 50.4 miles (81.2 km) trail leading through rural villages and past historical sites like the castle in Almenar de Soria, often completed in about 5 hours 8 minutes. Touring cycling around Gómara is defined by quiet rural roads, vast agricultural plains, and historical village backdrops. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging long-distance rides. The routes in Gómara are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 4 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Gómara's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Gómara?
A: The touring cycling routes around Gómara primarily traverse vast, diverse agricultural plains, characterized by cereal cultivation and open horizons. The terrain is generally flat or gently rolling, making it ideal for relaxed cycling. You'll also find low-lying mountain ranges like the Sierra de Costanazo in the distance, providing a scenic backdrop.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Gómara?
A: Yes, Gómara offers easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners or those seeking a relaxed ride. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31846497" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gómara Town Center loop from Gómara&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 7.7-mile (12.4 km) route. Another gentle option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150966" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almenar de Soria Castle – Almenar de Soria Castle loop from Almenar de Soria&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 4.6 miles (7.4 km) long.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes in the Gómara area?
A: While Gómara is known for its gentle terrain, there is a more challenging option for experienced touring cyclists. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041638642/almenar-de-soria-olvega-and-matalebreras-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almenar de Soria, Ólvega, and Matalebreras Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 50.4-mile (81.2 km) trail that takes you through rural villages and past historical sites, offering a longer and more demanding experience.

Q: Are the touring cycling routes around Gómara suitable for families?
A: Yes, the generally flat and gently rolling terrain of Gómara makes many of its routes suitable for families. The quiet rural roads and expansive agricultural plains provide a safe and pleasant environment for leisurely rides with children. Easy routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31846497" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gómara Town Center loop from Gómara&lt;/a&gt; are particularly well-suited for family outings.

Q: What historical sites can I explore while cycling near Gómara?
A: The region around Gómara is rich in history. While cycling, you can encounter remnants of castles, such as the well-preserved &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2683455" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almenar de Soria Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which is featured on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041638642/almenar-de-soria-olvega-and-matalebreras-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almenar de Soria, Ólvega, and Matalebreras Loop&lt;/a&gt;. You might also pass through charming villages with Romanesque churches, like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2683453" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ledesma de Soria&lt;/a&gt;, adding a cultural dimension to your ride.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Gómara?
A: Yes, there are several circular touring cycling routes available in Gómara. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31846497" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gómara Town Center loop from Gómara&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150966" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almenar de Soria Castle – Almenar de Soria Castle loop from Almenar de Soria&lt;/a&gt; are both circular routes, allowing you to start and end in the same location without retracing your steps.

Q: What kind of wildlife or natural features might I encounter on the routes?
A: While cycling through Gómara's agricultural plains, you'll experience vast open horizons. The region includes protected areas like the 'Altos de Gómara,' a Special Protection Area for Birds (ZEPA), known for steppe birds such as the Little Bustard and Pin-tailed Sandgrouse. The 'Quejigares de Gómara-Nágima' is also a Site of Community Importance (LIC) featuring gall oaks, offering opportunities for nature observation.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Gómara?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Gómara is generally during the spring and autumn months. These seasons offer pleasant temperatures and less extreme weather, making for comfortable rides across the open plains. Summer can be hot, and winter might bring colder conditions, though the flat terrain can still be accessible.

Q: Is there parking available for touring cyclists in Gómara?
A: Yes, Gómara, being a rural municipality, typically offers accessible parking in or near its town centers and villages. Cyclists can usually find suitable spots to park their vehicles before embarking on their touring cycling adventures. Specific parking areas might vary by village, but general street parking is often available.

Q: Can I reach Gómara by public transport with my bike?
A: Public transport options to Gómara, especially with bikes, might be limited given its rural location. While some regional bus services may operate, it's advisable to check with local transport providers in Soria for their specific policies regarding bicycles and schedules. Bringing your own vehicle might offer more flexibility for touring cyclists.

Q: Are there places to eat or drink along the touring cycling routes?
A: As you cycle through the villages and towns around Gómara, you will find local establishments such as cafes, bars, and small restaurants. These provide opportunities to refuel and experience local cuisine. It's a good idea to plan your route to pass through these settlements, especially on longer rides, to ensure you have access to refreshments.

Q: Where can I find scenic viewpoints on the touring cycling routes?
A: The expansive agricultural plains themselves offer panoramic views and open horizons, especially at sunrise or sunset. For specific scenic spots, look for routes that pass near the low-lying mountain ranges like the Sierra de Costanazo, La Pica, or Toranzo, which provide elevated perspectives of the Soria countryside. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041569047/santander-mediterranean-greenway-valcorba-ciria" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santander-Mediterranean Greenway: Valcorba - Ciria&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, traverses varied terrain including karst plateaus near Ciria, which can offer unique vistas.
tips: A small town located near the Santander-Mediterranean Greenway. Among its heritage, the church of Santa María la Mayor stands out, which presents the union of various architectural styles, including Gothic and Baroque, and the castle located on a rock.
This town has less than 10 inhabitants. Most of the houses are in ruins. The church is interesting and has a small cemetery next to it.
Although it is an important town, the truth is a bit disappointing. To highlight, the town hall square, the Church and the streets with arcades.

faqs.question: Is the Ciria from Gómara route suitable for beginner cyclists?
faqs.answer: While the overall difficulty is rated as 'moderate' due to its length, the trail primarily follows an old railway line, meaning it features generally flat terrain with gentle gradients. This makes it quite accessible for touring cyclists, including those with less experience, as the surface is typically compacted soil. It's part of the larger Santander-Mediterranean Greenway, known for its user-friendly paths.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery will I encounter on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: You can expect diverse and picturesque scenery. The route traverses farmlands around Gómara Town Center and transitions into more Mediterranean landscapes as it approaches the foothills of mountains like Aleza and Bigornia. Riders will pass through peaceful villages with traditional architecture, enjoying 'big skies, big rivers, countless tunnels and bridges, untouched forests and stunning local towns'.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Santander-Mediterranean Greenway near Gómara?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a rewarding experience throughout much of the year. For comfortable cycling, spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant temperatures and conditions. These seasons also showcase the changing landscapes beautifully, from blooming fields to rich autumnal colors.

faqs.question: Are there any historical features or points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, as a former railway line, the route incorporates historical infrastructure, including one tunnel and several viaducts and bridges. You'll also pass through charming settlements like Ciria and Portillo de Soria Village and Church, which offer glimpses into traditional architecture and local history. The region itself is rich in cultural significance.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Ciria from Gómara cycling route?
faqs.answer: Specific parking information for the starting point in Gómara is not detailed, but as a circular route, you can typically find parking within or near the Gómara Town Center or other villages along the greenway. Look for designated public parking areas or street parking where permitted.

faqs.question: Is the Ciria from Gómara — circular on the Santander-Mediterranean Greenway dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Greenways in Spain are generally dog-friendly, allowing leashed dogs to accompany cyclists. However, always ensure your dog is under control, especially when passing through villages or near farmlands. Be prepared to carry water for your pet, as specific water sources for animals may not be readily available along the entire route.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay any fees to use this greenway section?
faqs.answer: No, typically greenways (Vías Verdes) in Spain, including sections of the Santander-Mediterranean Greenway, do not require permits or charge entrance fees for public access. They are designed for free recreational use.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other greenways or paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is a significant part of the larger Santander-Mediterranean Greenway. Specifically, it covers a substantial portion of the Vía Verde Santander-Mediterráneo (Valcorba - Ciria) and other sections simply named Santander-Mediterráneo, which transforms disused railway lines into natural paths across Spain.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation near the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through peaceful villages like Ciria, Portillo de Soria Village and Church, and Gómara Town Center, you can expect to find local cafes, restaurants, and potentially small guesthouses or rural accommodation. The nearby city of Soria also offers numerous services.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a cycling trip on this greenway?
faqs.answer: For a comfortable cycling trip, bring plenty of water, snacks, sun protection (sunscreen, hat, sunglasses), and basic bike repair tools. Given the varied scenery and historical elements, a camera is also recommended. Wear appropriate cycling attire and consider layers, especially during spring and autumn.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Ciria from Gómara cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is generally easy, following an old railway line. This means it's mostly flat with gentle gradients, making it suitable for touring bicycles. The surface is typically compacted soil, providing a smooth ride for most users.
